track: 3 — recall/discovery (a mixed record: ONE genuine, structurally-verified IF-3a coupling fire recorded GATED + a clean Shape-B precision-silence across the other four families — on a new stack: Ruby on Rails, the first DYNAMIC-AUTOLOAD stack in the corpus)
shape: A+B — IF-3a FIRED (a real cross-directory Redis-key magic-string coupling, LOW–MEDIUM, recorded GATED / not filed) + IF-8/IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c stayed silent (0 genuine fires / 23 named suppressions total / 0 false defects). The signature Ruby finding is the AUTOLOAD-VISIBILITY LIMITATION: Zeitwerk resolves app/ dependencies by constant reference, not import statement, so IF-8 and IF-7c's import-graph moat have structurally REDUCED VISIBILITY on app/ code (an honest RECALL caveat, never an FP) — DeepInit declines to fabricate fires from edges it cannot ground.
engine_proxy: deterministic-detector-sweep (5 read-only agents — IF-8 / IF-6 / IF-10 / IF-7c / IF-3a — applying the SHIPPED deterministic predicates (§21/§24+§28/§25+§29/§23/AF-1) directly to the real Ruby source; NOT a full Detect→Emit pipeline; no cost ledger recorded). Build chain:  (5 agents). MAIN-LOOP VERIFICATION was decisive on this record: (a) the IF-3a Redis-key fire was independently re-verified line-by-line (the key string occurs in EXACTLY 2 places across app/+lib/+config — a write in a service, a read in a controller); (b) the IF-7c and IF-3a agents surfaced their findings but ran out of budget before finalizing StructuredOutput, so the main loop COMPLETED both adjudications — including catching + correcting a Windows-path methodology bug (`/c/tmp/...` returns an empty os.walk on native Windows Python, a false-negative trap) and re-running a careful rescue-classification scan (342 rescue clauses: 283 non-empty / 17 nil-fallback / 42 typed-expected-condition empty — 0 bare untyped).

repo.name: discourse/discourse
repo.pinned_sha_note: shallow-clone HEAD at 2026-06-12; the file:line citations are the durable anchors.
repo.license: GPL-2.0 (copyleft — DISCLOSED; DeepInit's use is read-only/local = non-derivative; the moonshot/upstream bar is EXTRA-hard for this repo). The relational data layer (Postgres/ActiveRecord) is the corpus's only true IF-2/ORM-drift target, but live-DB IF-2 stays ENVIRONMENT-PENDING (db_status=static_only — no Postgres run; A11), so IF-2 was not exercised here.
repo.stack: Ruby on Rails (the Discourse forum platform; Postgres/ActiveRecord, Zeitwerk autoloading) — the SEVENTH instrumented stack and the FIRST Ruby/Rails datapoint AND the first DYNAMIC-AUTOLOAD stack (prior: Crystal/kemal, Python/pyccel, TypeScript/excalidraw, Go/caddy, Rust/ripgrep, Java/Signal-Server)
repo.size: app/ + lib/ = ~204k Ruby LOC (scc). L-tier → COMPONENT-SCOPED.

analysis_scope: TWO scopes (mirrors the Java/Rust/Go records' whole-graph-IF-8 + scoped-deep-families split): (1) IF-8 over the DECIDABLE substrate = lib/'s explicit require_relative + load-path component graph (698 files → 270 graph nodes / 36 cross-component edges; component = top-level lib/ submodule) PLUS an honest assessment of the app/models autoload-implicit association graph. (2) IF-6 / IF-10 / IF-7c / IF-3a over the app-layer CORE = app/models (363 files / 47,502 LOC) + app/services (186 / 19,599) + app/controllers (137 / 24,691) = 686 files / 91,792 LOC (the request→domain→persistence path; component = the top-level app/ directory).
result_headline: Track-3 RUBY/RAILS field sweep over discourse/discourse@420bced6 (lib/ 698 files for the IF-8 graph; app core 686 files / ~91.8k LOC for the deep families): IF-3a FIRED on a genuine cross-directory shared-resource coupling — the Redis key string `"discourse_id_challenge_token"` is WRITTEN by app/services (challenge_flow.rb:41 setex) and READ by app/controllers (metadata_controller.rb:33 get), coupled ONLY by a bare duplicated magic-string with no mediating constant (LOW–MEDIUM, recorded GATED / not filed). The other four families stayed correctly SILENT: 0 genuine fires across IF-8/IF-6/IF-10/IF-7c with 23 named structural suppressions total (IF-8 4 / IF-6 5 / IF-10 4 / IF-7c 4 / IF-3a 6) and 0 false defects. The decisive Ruby-specific comprehension: Zeitwerk AUTOLOADING resolves app/ dependencies by constant reference (not import statement), so IF-8 and IF-7c's import-graph moat have structurally reduced visibility on app/ code — an honest RECALL limitation (e.g. the real Post↔Topic↔User model cycle is grounded but below the import substrate, and DeepInit declines to fabricate it into a fire), and Rails' MVC layering concentrates value-sets in the model layer (24 enums all in app/models) so cross-component IF-6 is structurally thin.

fires.title: Cross-directory shared Redis-key coupling via a bare duplicated magic-string (the discourse-id domain-verification handshake)
fires.severity_reason: an auxiliary discourse-id domain-verification integration, not the core request path; but a Core-adjacent identity handshake.
fires.certainty: HIGH (the coupling is structural + grounded: the key string occurs in EXACTLY 2 places across app/+lib/+config)
fires.read_site: app/controllers/metadata_controller.rb:33  —  token = Discourse.redis.get("discourse_id_challenge_token")  (in discourse_id_challenge, served at the public .well-known/discourse-id-challenge endpoint)
fires.coupling: the two sites are coupled ONLY by the duplicated bare string literal — NO shared constant, NO mediating method/interface. A typo or rename on one side silently breaks the domain-verification handshake (the controller reads nil → Discourse::NotFound). This is the textbook IF-3a 'change one, break the other' shape.
fires.contrast_evidence: a nearby CHALLENGE_KEY constant IS properly factored (a shared constant within one directory) — which by contrast highlights that the discourse_id token key is NOT factored, the exact distinction the fire rests on.
fires.recorded_disposition: GATED / reported, not filed (field-sweep discipline + the moonshot gate ladder NOT run; GPL-2.0 copyleft raises the upstream bar). A real, grounded, LOW–MEDIUM coupling — an architectural code-smell (extract a shared constant), not a runtime defect. Parallel to caddy's lone LOW IF-3a fire ().

suppressed.candidate: @@class variables (28 in app/models, 2 in app/services, 3 in app/controllers)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: single-component (>=2 DISTINCT directories required)
suppressed.reason: every @@ classvar is confined to ONE class within ONE directory — e.g. Category's @@subcategory_ids / @@cache_text / @@url_cache (DistributedCache / LruRedux memoization caches, category.rb:400-1023). A per-class cache is not a cross-directory shared mutable resource; the ≥2-component arm is FALSE.

suppressed.candidate: user-lifecycle cascade raw writes (user_destroyer / user_merger / anonymize_user / destroy_task — update_all/delete_all across many user-owned tables)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: reached through a typed interface (the AR model class) + recognized dependent-destroy cascade
suppressed.reason: these bulk operations sweep across many tables for performance, but each write goes THROUGH its ActiveRecord model class (the typed interface the predicate names as 'an explicit interface'). The recognized Rails dependent-destroy/merge/anonymize cascade pattern, not un-mediated silent coupling.

suppressed.candidate: CategoryUser / TagUser raw insert_all from model + site_setting service + groups_controller
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: reached through a rich typed domain API (CategoryUser.batch_set / set_notification_level_for_category / auto_track)
suppressed.reason: the same notification-state is bulk-backfilled from 3 sites, but ALL write through the CategoryUser model class, which exposes a rich domain interface (batch_set, set_notification_level_for_category). Normal per-user changes go through that API; the insert_all sites are performance backfills writing through the same model class → mediated, not silent. The predicate's 'typed method API = the explicit interface' suppress.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: not-a-global (string interpolation / JSON-schema $ref)
suppressed.reason: every '$...' hit in the app core is a STRING literal ($pbkdf2 hash prefix, JSON-schema "$ref"/"$defs"), not a Ruby global variable assignment. ZERO real $globals exist in the core. No shared mutable global resource.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: request-isolated (set+read within one request lifecycle), not cross-component temporal coupling
suppressed.reason: Thread.current[:_method_profiler] is per-request instrumentation context, set and read within a single request by the profiling middleware — request-scoped, not a persistent shared resource two components mutate across lifecycles.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: the DECLARED interface (not silent coupling)
suppressed.reason: associations ARE the explicit, declared relational interface between models — the opposite of an un-mediated shared resource. Never an IF-3a fire (and the non-double-emit guard with IF-5/IF-8 holds).

per_family.ruby_specific_note: IF-3a is the richest family for Ruby — the language is VERY permissive about mutable shared state (class variables @@, $globals, Thread.current, mutable constants) — and it produced the record's ONE genuine fire, exactly as predicted (the most over-fire-prone family on a permissive language surfaces a real low-severity coupling, like caddy's Go IF-3a fire). The fire is a bare duplicated Redis-key magic-string spanning app/services (write) and app/controllers (read) with no shared constant — a clean, grounded LOW–MEDIUM coupling. Everything else suppressed by a NAMED clause: Ruby's @@class-var caches are per-class single-component; the big raw-write candidates (user-lifecycle cascades, CategoryUser/TagUser backfills) all write THROUGH their ActiveRecord model class (the typed interface the predicate explicitly excludes — the AR model IS the mediating contract); $globals are all string-interpolation false hits; Thread.current is request-isolated; associations are the declared interface. The precision discipline held against the most permissive shared-state surface in the corpus: 1 grounded fire, 6 named suppression groups, 0 false defects.

suppressed.candidate: lib/ explicit-require component graph (270 nodes / 36 cross-component edges)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: acyclic chain (no back-edge) — Tarjan SCC = 0 SCCs of size>=2
suppressed.reason: the real Tarjan over the lib/ require_relative + load-path graph (53 directory submodules + 244 loose top-level files = 270 components, 36 cross-component edges) returns ZERO SCCs of size >= 2 — a strict DAG. CRITICAL HONESTY: this is NOT language-enforced (Ruby permits cycles, like Java; UNLIKE Go's 'import cycle not allowed' + Cargo's 'cyclic package dependency' bans) — it is the EXPLICIT-require subset being acyclic by maintainer discipline.

suppressed.candidate: plugin -> auth + discourse -> plugin (the most cycle-prone explicit chain)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: no back-edge (auth/plugin do not require back up the chain)
suppressed.reason: discourse->plugin->auth looks like it might loop (auth is central, discourse the umbrella), but no reciprocal require closes it → acyclic.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: no back-edge
suppressed.reason: directory-submodule A requires B with no B->A return edge → not a 2-node cycle.

suppressed.candidate: app/models implicit association cycle Post <-> Topic <-> User (a REAL class-level cycle)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: below the import substrate — autoload-visibility limitation (NOT a fabricated fire)
suppressed.reason: the cycle genuinely EXISTS and is grounded (Post belongs_to :topic [post.rb:33] while Topic has_many :posts [topic.rb:252]; User has_many :posts [user.rb:17] while Post belongs_to :user) — but it is INVISIBLE to IF-8's grep-import substrate because Zeitwerk resolves these by CONSTANT REFERENCE / association DSL, with NO require statement to map to an edge. The precision discipline holds: DeepInit does NOT fabricate the association graph into an IF-8 fire (associations are the DECLARED interface). This is the AUTOLOAD-VISIBILITY LIMITATION — an honest RECALL caveat, not an FP.

per_family.ruby_specific_note: Ruby/Rails is the first DYNAMIC-AUTOLOAD stack in the corpus and it splits IF-8 into TWO regimes. (1) The DECIDABLE lib/ substrate: like Java (and unlike Go/Cargo), Ruby imposes no dependency-cycle ban, so mutual require would be legal — the clean DAG here (270 nodes / 36 cross-component edges / 0 SCCs) is maintainer discipline, not a compiler guarantee. (2) The DOMINANT mechanism is ZEITWERK AUTOLOADING: app/ classes reference each other by bare constant + association DSL, resolved at runtime, with essentially ZERO explicit require between app/models (verified: app/models' only requires hit lib/ or gems, never another model). The genuine Post↔Topic↔User class-level cycle EXISTS and is grounded, but is below IF-8's import-graph substrate (no import statement) — the AUTOLOAD-VISIBILITY LIMITATION. So a 0-fire here reads as 'no cycle in the explicit-require lib graph', NOT 'no cycles in the app'. This is the honest Ruby-specific finding: on idiomatic Rails, IF-8 has structurally REDUCED VISIBILITY (the cyclic half is below the substrate); grounding it would need Graphify (a constant-reference/call-graph substrate), the coverage-matrix '°' caveat. Distinct from Java (Signal-Server), where imports are EXPLICIT so IF-8 found a real 33-package SCC; on Ruby the same kind of cycle is present but unobservable to the import substrate.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: same-component (all in app/models) + zero-overlap homonym + no behavioral entailment
suppressed.reason: 5 models define enum :status but ALL live in app/models (one component) → same-component suppress before any set-diff; and they are unrelated per-model lifecycles (media-download status vs reviewable status) with no cross-model value-flow.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: same-component + zero-overlap homonym
suppressed.reason: both in app/models, share no members → same-component AND unrelated.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: same-component
suppressed.reason: both in app/models → same-component suppress (and a non-empty diff between low/high and low/medium/high would be intra-component no-redeclare territory anyway).

suppressed.candidate: PRIORITIES constant (the ONLY cross-DIRECTORY value-set name found)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: behavioral-entailment FALSE (the load-bearing clause)
suppressed.reason: this is the ONLY same-canonical-name value-set spanning >=2 distinct directories with a genuine non-empty symmetric difference — so it is the one case that exercised the full predicate. Suppressed by behavioral entailment: a search-RANKING scale (searchable.rb) vs an admin-dashboard problem-SEVERITY set (problem_check) are unrelated subsystems with ZERO cross-component value-flow — exactly the clause that separates IF-6 from a naive same-name clone detector.

suppressed.candidate: frozen value-set constants across services/controllers (STATUSES / RESULT_FILTER_STATUSES / RESULT_TYPES / MEMBER_TYPES …)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: no cross-component collision (each canonical name appears in only one directory)
suppressed.reason: ~45 frozen-array/Set constants spread across the layers, but bucketed by exact canonical name, none (besides PRIORITIES) appears in >=2 directories → no symmetric difference to compute.

per_family.ruby_specific_note: FIRST Ruby datapoint for IF-6, and it surfaced a clean Rails STRUCTURAL fact: NAMED value-sets are overwhelmingly ActiveRecord `enum :name, {...}` declarations, and all 24 enums live EXCLUSIVELY in app/models (ZERO in app/services or app/controllers — verified). Because IF-6's component = top-level app/ directory, every same-named enum (status×5, type×2, priority×2) is SAME-COMPONENT by construction → suppressed before any set-diff. Cross-component IF-6 therefore rests entirely on frozen-array/Set constants (~45, which DO spread across the layers), of which exactly ONE name (PRIORITIES) appears in >=2 directories with a non-empty symmetric difference — and it is held back precisely by the BEHAVIORAL-ENTAILMENT clause (search-ranking scale vs admin problem-severity = unrelated subsystems). Net: Rails MVC layering structurally CONCENTRATES value-sets in the model layer, so cross-component IF-6 has genuinely reduced visibility on idiomatic Rails — an honest structurally-suppressed Shape-B, and a datapoint on how convention-over-configuration interacts with a cross-component detector. 0 fires / 5 named suppression groups / 0 false defects.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: no substrate (the IF-10 anchor does not exist)
suppressed.reason: an exhaustive scan of the ~92k-LOC app core (363 models + 186 services + 137 controllers) found ZERO `CONST = true|false` declarations (with or without .freeze). Discourse config is uniformly runtime-computed: SiteSetting.* (DB-backed), Rails.env.*, ENV[...], defined?(...) gem-presence — never a compile-time literal const flag. Nothing to fold.

suppressed.candidate: MINI_PROFILER_CLASS (the ONLY bare-const whole-if-test in the entire scope)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: non-literal RHS (a defined?-gated gem class, not a boolean literal)
suppressed.reason: the single `unless CONST` whole-test in ~92k LOC — but MINI_PROFILER_CLASS is assigned from a defined?(...) gem-presence check, not a literal → never folds.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: mixed condition (the const sits behind a `>` / `==` comparison — not the bound name alone)
suppressed.reason: the two literal constants that DO appear in ternaries are mixed conditions (a runtime operand co-decides), never the sole whole-test → no const-fold.

suppressed.candidate: 305 string/int/symbol literal constants used as lookup tables (CONST.include? / .match? / [k] / .keys.include?)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: not a whole if-test (consumed via method calls)
suppressed.reason: Ruby truthiness means a const of any type COULD gate an if, but these 305 literal consts serve as canonical values/keys/limits/regexes consumed through method calls — lookup tables, not whole if-tests. The LIKE SQL-fragment const (post.rb:153 etc.) appears inside string literals, never as an if-test.

per_family.ruby_specific_note: Ruby/Rails STARVES the IF-10 const-gated dead-branch predicate even more than Java did. The canonical shape (`CONST = true; if CONST`) needs a boolean-literal module-level constant, and the ~92k-LOC app core has ZERO of them — config is uniformly runtime-computed (SiteSetting DB-backed / Rails.env / ENV / defined? gem-presence). There ARE 305 string/int/symbol literal constants, but they are lookup tables consumed via method calls (CONST.include?/.match?/[k]), never whole if-tests. Ruby truthiness means any const COULD gate an if, yet a LITERAL const used as the SOLE whole if-test is essentially absent in idiomatic Rails: the one bare-const whole-test (MINI_PROFILER_CLASS) is non-literal-RHS (defined?-gated), and the two literal-const ternaries are mixed conditions. Net: IF-10 is structurally near-impossible on idiomatic Rails core code — a clean Shape-B-style 0-fire, comparable to ripgrep's clean sweep but for a DIFFERENT reason (config-as-runtime-state, not a language ban). 0 fires / 4 named suppression groups / 0 false defects.

suppressed.category: 283 NON-EMPTY rescue handlers (log / raise / return a default / do work)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: non-empty body — deliberate error-as-value (the slice keys STRICTLY on empty/comment-only)
suppressed.reason: of 342 block-form rescue clauses in the app core, 283 have a non-empty body (Rails.logger.warn / raise / return a default / set state) → deliberate handling, never a swallow.

suppressed.predicate_false_clause: deliberate error-as-value (returns nil as the explicit default)
suppressed.reason: a body of just `nil` returns nil on error — a deliberate value-fallback (e.g. `admin_dashboard_*.rb` `rescue ArgumentError, TypeError; nil`), the §23 'returns an explicit value' suppress.

suppressed.category: 42 typed-expected-condition EMPTY/comment-only swallows (0 BARE untyped rescue)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: deliberate error-as-value (typed begin/rescue-as-EXPRESSION returning nil) — AND the cross-component moat is unestablishable on autoload
suppressed.reason: ALL 42 empty/comment-only candidates are TYPED `rescue SpecificError` (0 bare untyped `rescue` exist in the core), and the dominant pattern is the begin/rescue-as-EXPRESSION returning nil on a typed expected error: `uri = begin URI(x) rescue URI::Error end` (embeddable_host.rb:21), `cdn_uri = begin URI.parse(x) rescue URI::Error end` (topic_link_click.rb:48), `cache_time = Time.rfc2822(x) rescue ArgumentError end` (stylesheets_controller.rb:50), `File.delete(p) rescue Errno::ENOENT end` (handle_chunk_upload.rb:49). These are deliberate, typed, value-producing fallbacks (the assignment receives nil), NOT silent operation-result swallows hiding a failure from a caller.

suppressed.category: the cross-component-consumption MOAT is unestablishable on Zeitwerk autoload (the decisive deterministic gate)
suppressed.predicate_false_clause: moat-blind on autoload — IF-7c never fires what it cannot ground (precision-safe / recall-limited)
suppressed.reason: even setting aside the error-as-value classification, §23 fires ONLY when the enclosing public method is CROSS-COMPONENT-CONSUMED (called from another directory), resolved via the import graph. On Rails the wiring between app/models↔services↔controllers is CONSTANT REFERENCES (autoload), not require statements, so DeepInit's grep-import substrate (which sees require/require_relative, concentrated in lib/) cannot ground the moat for app/ methods → the slice SUPPRESSES (precision-safe). A naive empty-rescue linter (RuboCop Lint/SuppressedException) flags all 42 with no moat; IF-7c's discipline is to require the cross-boundary fact it cannot cheaply establish here — the recall cost of precision on autoload (the same '°' caveat as IF-8, resolvable with Graphify).

per_family.ruby_specific_note: FIRST Ruby datapoint for IF-7c, and the main loop completed + verified the adjudication (the agent surfaced the data but ran out of budget — and notably caught a Windows-path methodology bug: `/c/tmp/...` returns an empty os.walk on native Windows Python, an initial false-negative that was corrected). The careful re-scan: 342 block-form rescue clauses in the app core → 283 non-empty + 17 nil-fallback (both §23 error-as-value suppress) + 42 empty/comment-only swallow candidates. The decisive Ruby facts: (1) ZERO bare untyped `rescue` exist in the core — every empty candidate is TYPED `rescue SpecificError`, and the dominant shape is the begin/rescue-as-EXPRESSION returning nil on a typed expected error (URI::Error / Errno::ENOENT / ArgumentError) — a deliberate value-fallback, the polarity-opposite of a silent swallow. (2) The §23 cross-component-consumption MOAT is unestablishable on Zeitwerk-autoloaded app/ code (constant references, not requires, wire the directories), so the deterministic slice correctly stays SILENT — precision-safe, recall-limited (the autoload-visibility limitation, IF-7c's Ruby '°' caveat, resolvable with Graphify). Two independent reasons suppress every candidate → 0 fires / 0 false defects.

naive_vs_guarded_illustrative.guarded_fire_disposition: GATED (the IF-3a Redis-key coupling — a real LOW–MEDIUM code-smell, recorded not filed)
naive_vs_guarded_illustrative.note: A naive surface detector would over-fire on ~155 near-candidates across the five families: IF-3a ~50 (every @@classvar + raw update_all/insert_all + Thread.current a 'shared global' matcher would flag), IF-8 ~4 (the implicit Post↔Topic↔User cycle + the cross-submodule lib edges a naive graph would chase or — worse — fabricate from associations), IF-6 ~9 (the same-named enums + frozen consts a bare name-keyed set-diff would fire on), IF-10 ~50 (every `if CONST`/`unless CONST` a const-looking-token matcher would chase, all runtime/lookup-table consts), IF-7c ~42 (every empty/comment-only rescue a no-empty linter flags, with NO cross-boundary moat). The guarded predicates resolve all of them to ONE honestly-disclosed grounded fire (the Redis-key coupling, gated) and ZERO false defects — each suppression names the load-bearing clause, and critically the slice declines to fabricate fires from the autoload-implicit edges it cannot ground (the recall cost of precision).

key_ruby_findings: AUTOLOAD-VISIBILITY LIMITATION (the signature finding, spanning IF-8 + IF-7c): Zeitwerk resolves app/ dependencies by CONSTANT REFERENCE, not import statement, so IF-8's cycle detection and IF-7c's cross-component-consumption moat are BLIND to app-layer wiring. The real Post↔Topic↔User model cycle is grounded but below the import substrate, and IF-7c's moat cannot be established for autoloaded methods. DeepInit declines to fabricate fires from these implicit edges — an honest RECALL caveat (resolvable with Graphify, the coverage-matrix '°'), NEVER an FP. Distinct from Java (Signal-Server), where EXPLICIT imports let IF-8 find a real 33-package SCC.

IF-3a FIRED (the richest family on a permissive language): the Redis key string "discourse_id_challenge_token" couples app/services (write, setex) and app/controllers (read, get) via a bare duplicated magic-string with no shared constant — a clean LOW–MEDIUM cross-directory coupling, recorded GATED. Parallel to caddy's Go IF-3a fire. Everything else suppressed by a named clause: @@class-var caches are per-class single-component; user-lifecycle cascades + CategoryUser/TagUser backfills write THROUGH the AR model class (the typed interface); $globals are string-interpolation false hits; Thread.current is request-isolated; associations are the declared interface.

Rails MVC concentrates value-sets in the MODEL layer → cross-component IF-6 is structurally thin: all 24 ActiveRecord enums live in app/models (same-component), so cross-component IF-6 rests on frozen constants, of which exactly ONE name (PRIORITIES) spans >=2 directories — held back by behavioral entailment (search-ranking vs admin problem-severity, unrelated subsystems). A datapoint on convention-over-configuration vs a cross-component detector.

Config-as-runtime-state STARVES IF-10 (even more than Java): ZERO boolean-literal constants in the ~92k-LOC app core — config is SiteSetting (DB) / Rails.env / ENV / defined? gem-presence, never a `CONST = true` literal flag. The 305 literal consts are lookup tables (CONST.include?/[k]), never whole if-tests. Clean Shape-B for a different reason than the language bans.

0 BARE untyped rescue in the entire app core: every empty/comment-only rescue (42 of 342) is a TYPED `rescue SpecificError` deliberate expected-condition swallow — mostly begin/rescue-as-expression returning nil (URI::Error / Errno::ENOENT / ArgumentError) — the §23 error-as-value polarity-opposite of a silent swallow. A discipline datapoint for the platform.
honest_assessment: This is the SEVENTH instrumented stack, the FIRST Ruby/Rails datapoint, and the FIRST dynamic-autoload stack in the corpus — and it sharpens the cross-language story in two ways. (1) Like Java (and unlike Go/Rust), Ruby imposes no dependency-cycle ban; but UNLIKE Java's explicit imports, Rails' Zeitwerk autoloading makes app/ dependencies IMPLICIT constant references, so IF-8 and IF-7c's import-graph moat have structurally reduced visibility on app/ code — the real Post↔Topic↔User cycle and the cross-component rescue consumption are present but below the substrate, and DeepInit correctly DECLINES to fabricate them into fires (an honest recall caveat, the precise opposite of a false positive — and the strongest demonstration yet of the precision-first discipline: silence where it cannot ground a fact, not a guess). (2) The most over-fire-prone family on the most permissive shared-state language (IF-3a) surfaced exactly ONE genuine, grounded LOW–MEDIUM coupling (a bare duplicated Redis-key magic-string across services↔controllers, recorded GATED) while suppressing every larger candidate by a named clause — most tellingly the user-lifecycle cascades and CategoryUser/TagUser backfills, which write through their ActiveRecord model class (the typed interface), and the @@class-var caches, which are per-class single-component. Rails' MVC layering concentrates value-sets in the model layer (thinning cross-component IF-6) and its config-as-runtime-state starves IF-10 (zero boolean-literal const flags). The main loop's verification was load-bearing on this record: it independently re-verified the IF-3a fire, caught + corrected a Windows-path methodology bug that had produced a false-negative IF-7c count, and completed the IF-7c/IF-3a adjudications the agents ran out of budget on. Net: 1 genuine GATED fire (IF-3a coupling) + 0 false defects + 23 named suppressions across all five cross-module families — a high-precision, mechanism-grounded result that adds Ruby/Rails (a no-cycle-ban, autoload, AR/Postgres, config-as-runtime stack) as the 7th instrumented stack and makes the autoload-visibility limitation an explicit, honestly-scoped recall caveat.

totals.genuine_fire_disposition: GATED (IF-3a Redis-key coupling, LOW–MEDIUM — reported, not filed)
totals.counts_note: Counts summed from the structured suppressed[] arrays (IF-8/IF-6/IF-10 finalized by their agents; IF-3a fire + suppressions and IF-7c suppressions completed + verified by the main loop after the agents ran out of budget). IF-7c's 4 suppression CATEGORIES cover all 342 rescue clauses (283 non-empty + 17 nil-fallback + 42 typed-expected-condition + the autoload-moat clause). Naive-overfire ≈155 across families; guarded → 1 gated fire + 0 false defects.
